scenario_spec.signal_type: narrowband far-field two-source ULA DoA estimation with subspace pseudo-spectrum localization

core_parameters.resolution_success_rule: exactly two dominant peaks, each within +/-2 deg of its nearest true DoA
core_parameters.threshold_gap_rule: threshold region flagged when RMSE exceeds 3x stochastic CRB

test_scenarios.name: nominal_snr_sweep
test_scenarios.description: Evaluate RMSE, pseudo-spectrum behavior, and CRB ratio over SNR 0:2:30 dB with fixed M=8, L=200, and true angles [-7.5, 7.5] deg.

test_scenarios.name: resolution_probability_curve
test_scenarios.description: Estimate probability of resolution versus SNR using the provided two-peak +/-2 deg success rule and extract minimum SNR achieving probability >= 0.9.

test_scenarios.name: single_snr_pseudospectrum_diagnostics
test_scenarios.description: At nominal SNR 20 dB, generate trial-level pseudo-spectrum and peak separation statistics for localization sanity checks.

expected_outputs: theta_hat_deg[snr_index, trial, D] for each estimator

scenario_spec.generation_notes: Use wavelength-normalized steering vectors, enforce per-sensor SNR scaling, and reuse the same trial realizations across estimators for fair comparison.

system_model_doc: The snapshot model is \(\mathbf{x}[\ell]=\mathbf{A}(\boldsymbol{\theta})\mathbf{s}[\ell]+\mathbf{n}[\ell]\in\mathbb{C}^{M}\), \(\ell=1,\dots,L\), with \(M=8\), \(D=2\), ULA spacing \(d=\lambda/2\), and steering vector \(\mathbf{a}(\theta)=[1,e^{-j\pi\sin\theta},\dots,e^{-j\pi(M-1)\sin\theta}]^T\); sources are mutually uncorrelated and noise is spatially white \(\mathcal{CN}(\mathbf{0},\sigma_n^2\mathbf{I})\), so \(\mathbf{R}_x=\mathbf{A}\mathbf{R}_s\mathbf{A}^H+\sigma_n^2\mathbf{I}\) has rank-\(D\) signal subspace and orthogonal noise subspace exploited by MUSIC, with ULA shift-invariance also enabling Root-MUSIC and ESPRIT baselines.

math_formulation.problem_type: parametric subspace DoA estimation with spectral peak localization and threshold characterization

objective.description: Estimate the ordered DoA vector and pseudo-spectrum from finite snapshots, then evaluate RMSE and resolution probability versus SNR against stochastic CRB.

variables.name: number of sensors
variables.description: ULA element count with fixed value 8.

variables.name: number of snapshots
variables.description: Temporal snapshots per trial with fixed value 200.

variables.name: number of sources
variables.description: Known model order with fixed value 2.

variables.name: array manifold
variables.description: Vandermonde steering matrix of the ULA.

variables.name: true and sample covariance
variables.description: Population and finite-sample spatial covariance matrices.

variables.name: noise subspace basis
variables.description: Eigenvectors associated with the smallest \(M-D\) eigenvalues.

variables.name: MUSIC pseudo-spectrum
variables.description: Reciprocal projection energy on the estimated noise subspace.

variables.name: per-sensor input SNR
variables.description: Signal-to-noise ratio used in sweep 0 to 30 dB.

constraints.description: Sources are mutually uncorrelated and noise is spatially white.
Noise-subspace orthogonality creates pseudo-spectrum peaks at true DoAs.
Resolution threshold is the minimum SNR meeting target resolution probability.

key_formulas.name: Stochastic CRB
key_formulas.description: Fisher-information-based stochastic bound for covariance-parameterized DoA.

key_formulas.name: Resolution probability
key_formulas.description: Monte Carlo success rate under the provided two-peak rule.

assumptions: Sources are narrowband, far-field, and temporally i.i.d. across snapshots.
Source count D=2 is known for all subspace methods.
Array calibration is ideal with no mutual coupling or position errors.
SNR is consistently defined per sensor input for simulation and bounds.

model_properties.convexity: Non-convex in DoA parameters with grid-based spectral search for MUSIC and polynomial rooting for Root-MUSIC.
model_properties.special_structure: Vandermonde ULA manifold, rank-2 signal subspace, noise-subspace orthogonality, shift-invariance (ESPRIT), and approximately Toeplitz covariance.

math_formulation.formulation_markdown: Given \(\hat{\mathbf{R}}_x\) from \(L=200\) snapshots of an \(M=8\) half-wavelength ULA, estimate \(\boldsymbol{\theta}\in[-\pi/2,\pi/2]^2\) by exploiting the rank-\(D\) eigenspace split \(\hat{\mathbf{R}}_x=\mathbf{U}_s\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_s\mathbf{U}_s^H+\mathbf{U}_n\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_n\mathbf{U}_n^H\) and the orthogonality \(\mathbf{a}(\theta_i)^H\mathbf{U}_n\approx 0\), then localize peaks of \(P_{\mathrm{MU}}(\theta)\) on the scan grid to obtain \(\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}\); evaluate \(\mathrm{RMSE}(\mathrm{SNR})\), \(P_{\mathrm{res}}(\mathrm{SNR})\), and \(\mathrm{RMSE}/\mathrm{CRB}\) over 0:2:30 dB and define the resolution threshold as the minimum SNR where \(P_{\mathrm{res}}\ge 0.9\).

design_implications: Vandermonde ULA and rank-2 covariance justify subspace estimators as primary low-complexity choices.
Noise-subspace orthogonality directly enables MUSIC/Root-MUSIC peak localization and resolution analysis.
Shift-invariance structure motivates ESPRIT as a gridless cross-check baseline.
Approximate Toeplitz covariance can be used for optional covariance denoising sensitivity tests.
evaluation_focus: Primary curve is DoA RMSE versus SNR with identical Monte Carlo trials across estimators.
Stochastic CRB is the mandatory benchmark overlay for RMSE-to-bound ratio and threshold-gap marking.
Resolution threshold is reported as minimum SNR where empirical resolution probability reaches 0.9.
Low-SNR breakdown interpretation may be WWB-informed while CRB remains the required reference.
implementation_risks: Finite-snapshot subspace leakage can cause missed or merged pseudo-spectrum peaks near threshold SNR.
Peak-picking heuristics and edge effects can bias resolution probability unless robust local-max filtering is used.
Inconsistent SNR scaling between simulation and CRB computation can invalidate bound comparisons.
ML baselines may be runtime-heavy and sensitive to initialization or grid density in notebook settings.

proposed_algorithm.name: Grid-MUSIC with Monte Carlo SNR sweep and CRB overlay
proposed_algorithm.summary: Compute sample-covariance eigenspaces per trial, evaluate MUSIC pseudo-spectrum on a fixed angle grid, pick two dominant peaks, and aggregate RMSE/resolution statistics versus SNR.

algorithm_steps.name: Build angle grid and true manifold
algorithm_steps.description: Create scan grid and true steering matrix used for data generation and spectral evaluation.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use np.arange for grid and np.exp with broadcasting over sensor index m and angle arrays.

algorithm_steps.name: Loop over SNR and trials with power scaling
algorithm_steps.description: For each SNR point and Monte Carlo trial, set source/noise variances to match per-sensor input SNR.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Convert dB via 10**(snr_db/10.0) and keep loops over q,t for reproducible Monte Carlo.

algorithm_steps.name: Generate random source and noise snapshots
algorithm_steps.description: Draw complex Gaussian source and noise matrices with required covariance.

algorithm_steps.name: Synthesize received snapshots
algorithm_steps.description: Form observation matrix from steering, sources, and noise.

algorithm_steps.name: Estimate sample covariance
algorithm_steps.description: Compute Hermitian covariance for subspace decomposition.

algorithm_steps.name: Eigen-decompose and sort subspaces
algorithm_steps.description: Extract ordered eigenpairs and isolate noise subspace dimension M-D.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use np.linalg.eigh then reverse order with np.argsort(eigvals)[::-1].

algorithm_steps.name: Evaluate MUSIC pseudo-spectrum on grid
algorithm_steps.description: Compute reciprocal noise-subspace projection energy for each scan angle.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Precompute steering matrix Agrid (M x G); use proj = np.sum(np.conj(Agrid)*(Un@Un.conj().T@Agrid), axis=0).

algorithm_steps.name: Peak picking and DoA estimation
algorithm_steps.description: Find local maxima and select the two strongest distinct peaks as DoA estimates.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use scipy.signal.find_peaks or boolean local-max test plus np.argpartition.

algorithm_steps.name: Per-trial error and resolution indicator
algorithm_steps.description: Compute ordered estimation error and success flag under the given peak rule.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Sort estimated/true angles and compute nearest-distance checks in radians.

algorithm_steps.name: Aggregate metrics across trials and SNR
algorithm_steps.description: Compute RMSE, resolution probability, threshold mask, and minimum SNR meeting target probability.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use np.mean, np.sqrt, and np.where for first-index threshold extraction.

algorithm_steps.name: Compute stochastic CRB curve
algorithm_steps.description: For each SNR, evaluate Fisher information and bound for the two-angle parameter vector.
algorithm_steps.numpy_hint: Use analytical dA/dtheta for ULA, then np.linalg.inv and trace products for 2x2 FIM.

baseline_algorithms.name: Root-MUSIC
baseline_algorithms.summary: Uses ULA polynomial rooting from the noise projector to avoid dense angular scanning.
baseline_algorithms.numpy_hint: Form polynomial coefficients from projector diagonals and use np.roots; select roots nearest unit circle.

baseline_algorithms.name: ESPRIT
baseline_algorithms.summary: Exploits ULA shift-invariance of the signal subspace to estimate phase shifts and DoAs.
baseline_algorithms.numpy_hint: Use np.linalg.pinv and np.linalg.eig on small DxD matrix.

baseline_algorithms.name: Deterministic Maximum Likelihood
baseline_algorithms.summary: Searches DoAs that minimize projection residual energy of snapshots.
baseline_algorithms.numpy_hint: Use 2D grid search for two angles with batched matrix operations.

baseline_algorithms.name: Stochastic Maximum Likelihood
baseline_algorithms.summary: Fits covariance parameters by Gaussian log-likelihood minimization over DoAs.
baseline_algorithms.numpy_hint: Implement alternating updates or nested grid for angles with closed-form nuisance updates where possible.

baseline_algorithms.name: Stochastic CRB for two-source ULA DoA estimation
baseline_algorithms.summary: Analytical lower bound used as mandatory benchmark curve across SNR.
baseline_algorithms.numpy_hint: analytical formula — compute and plot as reference

complexity.per_iteration: Per trial per SNR: O(M^3 + G M (M-D)) for eigendecomposition plus MUSIC scan; total O(Q T (M^3 + G M (M-D))).
complexity.dominant_operation: Hermitian eigendecomposition of the MxM sample covariance and dense pseudo-spectrum evaluation over G grid points.
